note: APPEND-ONLY. Each entry is one iteration of the LM line and renders as one module on /lm, newest at the bottom. Never edit or remove old entries; add new ones at the end.

modules.title: 1 Â· Character level, composition isolated
modules.desc: Char IDs in (one-hot), no vision confound. The genome stack composes cross-position conjunctions beyond the additive anchor. Best seed shown; multi-seed spread 0.386-0.406. Broke the bigram ceiling by +11 points on every seed.

modules.title: 2 Â· Glyph frames: vision + language at once
modules.desc: Stage-2 of the char line: every character rendered as a noisy 32x32 glyph frame; the temporal radial stack does perception AND sequence prediction simultaneously. Also broke the bigram ceiling.

modules.title: 3 Â· Word level, V=2000, SVD embeddings (fat head)
modules.desc: The pivot to words: SVD embeddings + identity one-hots + continuation tables, everything into a flat head. Strong numbers, wrong shape: 4.8M+ params, almost all head, 66 evolved. Samples: locally grammatical, falls into conversational-filler attractors.

modules.title: 4 Â· LEAN: genomes carry the model
modules.desc: Anchor removed from head and fitness base per the shape diagnosis. Genomes finally earn on language (334 frozen, 37 attend/content-addressing). 14x fewer params at 67% of fat accuracy.

modules.title: 5 Â· HYBRID: genomes + 256 semantic channels
modules.desc: Lean head plus only the continuation-embedding block (never the raw identity/probability channels). Recovers half the fat gap at 1.2M params.

modules.title: 6 Â· RS embedding space (5k words, 120MB wiki)
modules.desc: Evolution separates words instead of predicting: each frozen genome is one embedding dimension, fitness is separation quality on held-out profile halves. Beat the SVD gate 3x on this corpus; neighbors king->frederick/emperor/prince, said->told/stated/explained.

modules.title: 7 Â· Swap A/B: RS-5k embeddings, fat head
modules.desc: The evolved table replaces SVD. Fat head loses 8.4 top-1 - later shown to be almost entirely the 5k-vs-30k coverage confound, not semantics.

modules.title: 8 Â· Swap A/B: RS-5k embeddings, lean
modules.desc: Through composition the gap nearly closes: top-5 ties SVD-lean, and lean-RS recovers 98.8% of its own fat ceiling vs 82% for SVD. Evolved embeddings are more composable.

modules.title: 9 Â· RS embedding space rebuilt at 30k words
modules.desc: Coverage-confound probe: same corpus, same fitness, 30k vocabulary. The intrinsic spearman gate flipped against RS - and predicted nothing about task value. The task A/B is the real gate.

modules.title: 10 Â· RS-30k fat: evolved embeddings TIE SVD
modules.desc: With coverage matched, the evolved table ties SVD through the flat head (0.3296 vs 0.3276). The entire earlier gap was coverage. Evolution's embeddings equal SVD for linear use, from 120MB vs SVD's full corpus.

modules.title: 11 Â· RS-30k lean: BEST lean model - evolved embeddings beat SVD through composition
modules.desc: 0.2973 top-1 / 0.5009 top-5 at 1.09M params - beats SVD-lean by +3.0/+5.5 and reaches 90% of its fat ceiling in 3 spaces. Genomes compose better over structure evolution built.

modules.title: 12 · PROBE: where the lean model is blind
modules.desc: Read-only breakdown of the RS-30k lean checkpoint. The reveal: when the continuation table's own top-5 contains the target (62% of test) the model scores 0.4801; when it does not (38%) it scores 0.0040 - chance. The model never overrides its tables, and the words it misses most are the commonest ones (i, you, the, it), which wider context determines.

modules.title: 13 · CONTINUE-TRAIN on the blind spot - method works, repair does not
modules.desc: The animation Module-3 warm start ported to language: frozen 285-genome base, new spaces on a 3x table-miss-weighted mix, head refit on the true distribution. No forgetting (has-target class held), overall +0.2, but the miss class barely moved (0.0040 to 0.0056) and evolution went dry after 10 genomes. Finding: continue-training cannot conjure signal the environment does not carry - the miss class needs NEW environment statistics (skip-gram tables, syntax-position profiles), not more search over the same channels.

modules.title: 14 · SKIP-GRAM ENVIRONMENT + CONTINUE-TRAIN - the loop closes: 0.3992, best model of the line
modules.desc: Module 13 said the miss class needs new environment statistics. Added: skip-gram continuation tables keyed (w-3,w-1) and (w-3,w-2) - wider context the trigram tables cannot see - as 4,256 new channels, with a headroom diagnostic first (45.9% of blind windows answerable). Continue-trained the same frozen base: +65 genomes, 45s. TEST 0.2974 -> 0.3992 top-1, 0.5010 -> 0.5981 top-5; the blind class went 0.0040 -> 0.1106 (28x); every slice improved; no forgetting. This lean model now beats every fat head ever built here (best 0.3296) at ~1.1M params. The probe -> environment-gap -> continue-train loop is validated on language.

modules.title: 15 · SECOND CRANK: far skip-grams take it to 0.4832 - simple-neural-LM territory
modules.desc: The loop again, on the Module-14 model: rebuild verified (0.3987 vs 0.3992), far tables keyed (w-4,w-1)/(w-5,w-1) added, headroom 64.9% of remaining errors answerable. Continue-train: +178 genomes, 64s. TEST 0.3992 -> 0.4832 top-1, 0.5981 -> 0.6643 top-5. Blind class 0.1106 -> 0.2051; bigram-backoff 0.327 -> 0.476. Cumulative crank: 0.2974 -> 0.3992 -> 0.4832 at ~1.2M params, gradient-free.

modules.title: 16 · THIRD CRANK: 4-gram + far skips -> 0.5663 top-1 / 0.7280 top-5 - past the usefulness bar
modules.desc: True 4-gram table (last three words) + skips (w-6,w-1)/(w-5,w-2). Headroom: 84.5% of remaining errors answerable, quad alone 68%. Continue-train: +80 genomes, 32s. TEST 0.4832 -> 0.5663 top-1, 0.6643 -> 0.7280 top-5 - top-5 crosses the genuinely-useful band (60-70%). Blind class 0.2051 -> 0.2938. Cumulative crank: 0.2974 -> 0.3992 -> 0.4832 -> 0.5663 at ~1.2M params, gradient-free, one frozen base grown by continue-training.

modules.title: 17 · THE CURRICULUM begins - Stage A: letters. TEST 0.9960 in 7 seconds
modules.desc: The user's design: build models that learn English like a kid - letters, then words (fill-in-the-blank), then cloze phrases, then autoregression; each stage its own model or continue-trained on the previous. Stage A: identify single rendered letters (26 classes, sizes 14-26px, jitter, noise). 515 perception genomes, self-stopped at 2 spaces, TEST 0.9960 - gate PASSED. This is also the diagnosis of the sentence-strip failure: perception earns instantly on single glyphs; the vision line needed a childhood. Stage A's frozen genomes become Stage B's eye.

modules.title: 18 · Curriculum Stage B: words - the model learns to SPELL. 0.7066 top-1 / 0.9358 top-5
modules.desc: A word is a strip of 8 letter tiles; identify which of 500 words (from pixels only). Stage A's 515 letter genomes frozen as the eye. The measured story: the orderless letter-bag baseline (eye only, no order) scores 0.1910; the first composition space adds +45 points by putting letters IN ORDER - that gap is spelling. 297 new genomes, 30 seconds. Next gate: Stage C, cloze.

modules.title: 19 · Curriculum Stage C: cloze - GATE FAILED, and the failure is the lesson
modules.desc: Name the blanked word in a 4-word phrase (V=500, pixels only, A->B chain frozen as the per-slot word eye). Word-bag baseline 0.0608; evolution earned NOTHING on top (first space produced zero genomes); final 0.0668 top-1 / 0.2150 top-5 - 33x chance but no composition. The lesson: perception stages (letters, spelling) pass in seconds because their answers are deterministic in the pixels; the SEMANTIC stage has no answer in 20k phrases of pixels alone. A kid at this stage has years of heard language - distributional experience. The curriculum's next design decision: give Stage C 'listening experience' (corpus co-occurrence statistics as environment), scale the data by orders of magnitude, or both.

modules.title: 20 · Stage C2: the kid gets EARS - cloze doubles (0.1212) but the gate holds
modules.desc: The RS-evolved semantic space (evolution's own 30k-word layer) attached as 512 ear channels on the observed context words; the blank stays unknown. Cloze 0.0668 -> 0.1212 top-1 / 0.2684 top-5 (61x chance) - real signal, still no pass, and evolution again earned zero composition. The sharpened diagnosis: the ears carry SIMILARITY (what words mean like) but cloze needs SEQUENCE experience (what follows what). A kid's listening includes heard word-order. Next design question: an evolved continuation layer (RS-style, but separating contexts by what follows them) as the second ear.

modules.title: 21 · Stage C3: sequence ears - evolution EARNS on semantics for the first time. 0.1418 / 0.2952
modules.desc: Two new evolution-bred ears join: embed_rs_next (genomes separating words by what FOLLOWS them - numbers cluster perfectly, speech verbs together) and embed_rs_prev (by what PRECEDES - openly grammatical: past-tense verbs cluster, the-determiner nouns cluster). The ear ladder on cloze: deaf 0.0668 -> similarity 0.1212 -> +sequence 0.1418 top-1 / 0.2952 top-5 (71x chance) - and for the first time on the semantic stage, composition genomes froze (10). The gate is still shy of decisive; the named next lever: scale the directional ears (64 -> 128+ dims) or evolve pair-context continuation ears.

modules.title: 22 · C4: ear capacity ruled out + the ceiling that reframes the gate
modules.desc: Directional ears scaled 64->128 dims: 0.1384/0.2850 - slightly BELOW the 64-dim run. Capacity is not the bottleneck. Then the reference we never had: perfect symbolic tables (tri/bi/uni, vocab-restricted) score 0.2494 top-1 / 0.4728 top-5 on this exact cloze set. The evolution-made kid (0.1418/0.2952) runs at 57-62% of the table ceiling - from raw pixels and evolution-bred experience only. The gate question is now calibrated: is 60%-of-ceiling a pass, or do we close the gap (the kid also sees RIGHT context, which the tables cannot - headroom above the ceiling exists in principle).
